name: Improper Restriction of Operations within the Bounds of a Memory Buffer
description: The software performs operations on a memory buffer, but it can read from or write to a memory location that is outside of the intended boundary of the buffer.

name: Buffer Copy without Checking Size of Input
description: The program copies an input buffer to an output buffer without verifying that the size of the input buffer is less than the size of the output buffer.

name: Stack-based Buffer Overflow
description: A stack-based buffer overflow condition exists when the software writes data beyond the boundaries of a stack-allocated buffer.

name: Heap-based Buffer Overflow
description: A heap-based buffer overflow condition exists when the software writes data beyond the boundaries of a heap-allocated buffer.

name: Write-what-where Condition
description: Any condition where the attacker has the ability to write an arbitrary value to an arbitrary location, frequently as the result of a buffer overflow.

name: Buffer Underwrite
description: The software writes to a buffer using an index or pointer that references a memory location prior to the beginning of the buffer.

name: Out-of-bounds Read
description: The software reads data past the end, or before the beginning, of the intended buffer.

name: Buffer Over-read
description: The software reads from a buffer using an index or pointer that references a memory location after the end of the buffer.
code_examples: memcpy(dst, src, 100); // src is only 10 bytes

name: Buffer Under-read
description: The software reads from a buffer using an index or pointer that references a memory location prior to the beginning of the buffer.

name: Improper Validation of Array Index
description: The product uses untrusted input when calculating an array index, but the product does not validate or incorrectly validates the index.

name: Incorrect Calculation of Buffer Size
description: The software does not correctly calculate the size to be used when allocating a buffer, which could lead to a buffer overflow.

name: Use of Externally-Controlled Format String
description: The software uses a function that accepts a format string as an argument, but the format string originates from an external source.

name: Improper Null Termination
description: The software does not terminate a string with a null character, causing the string to be improperly terminated.
code_examples: strncpy(dst, src, 10); // dst may not be null-terminated

name: Integer Overflow or Wraparound
description: The software performs a calculation that can produce an integer overflow or wraparound, which could cause unexpected behavior.
code_examples: int total = a * b; // a*b exceeds INT_MAX

name: Integer Underflow
description: The software subtracts one value from another, resulting in an underflow where the result is less than expected.

name: Integer Coercion Error
description: Integer coercion occurs when an integer is implicitly cast to a smaller type, causing data loss or unexpected behavior.

name: Off-by-one Error
description: A product calculates or uses an incorrect maximum or minimum value that is 1 more, or 1 less, than the correct value.

name: Unexpected Sign Extension
description: The software performs an operation on a signed integer that causes it to be sign-extended when converted to a larger type.
code_examples: char c = -1; int i = c; // i becomes -1, not 255

name: Signed to Unsigned Conversion Error
description: The software converts a signed integer to an unsigned integer, which can produce unexpected values if the original value is negative.
code_examples: int len = -1; size_t s = len; // s becomes very large
strlen returns size_t but stored in int

name: Unsigned to Signed Conversion Error
description: The software converts an unsigned integer to a signed integer, which can produce unexpected negative values.
code_examples: size_t s = 0xFFFFFFFF; int i = s; // i becomes -1

name: Numeric Truncation Error
description: Truncation errors occur when a larger type is cast to a smaller type and the value exceeds the range of the smaller type.
code_examples: long l = INT_MAX+1L; int i = l;

name: Use of Inherently Dangerous Function
description: The program uses a function that can lead to security vulnerabilities if not used carefully.

name: Missing Release of Memory after Effective Lifetime
description: The software does not sufficiently track and release allocated memory after it has been used, resulting in a memory leak.
code_examples: void* p = malloc(100); return; // no free

name: Double Free
description: The product calls free() twice on the same memory address, which could lead to memory corruption or arbitrary code execution.

name: Use After Free
description: Referencing memory after it has been freed can cause a program to crash, use unexpected values, or execute code.

name: Use of Uninitialized Variable
description: The code uses a variable that has not been initialized, leading to unpredictable behavior.
code_examples: char buf[10]; strcpy(buf, src); // buf uninitialized

name: NULL Pointer Dereference
description: A NULL pointer dereference occurs when the application dereferences a pointer that it expects to be valid, but is NULL.

name: Return of Stack Variable Address
description: A function returns the address of a stack variable, which will become invalid once the function returns.
code_examples: char* f() { char buf[10]; return buf; }

name: Use of Potentially Dangerous Function
description: The program invokes a potentially dangerous function that could introduce a vulnerability.

name: Incorrect Conversion between Numeric Types
description: When converting from one data type to another, the conversion is performed incorrectly.
code_examples: float f = 1.5; int i = f; // truncation

name: Out-of-bounds Write
description: The software writes data past the end, or before the beginning, of the intended buffer.

name: Access of Memory Location After End of Buffer
description: The software reads or writes to a buffer using an index or pointer that references a memory location after the end of the buffer.

name: Buffer Access with Incorrect Length Value
description: The software uses a length value to access a buffer, but the length is incorrect.
code_examples: memcpy(dst, src, sizeof(src)); // should be sizeof(dst)

name: Buffer Access Using Size of Source Buffer
description: The software uses the size of a source buffer when accessing a destination buffer, which could cause a buffer overflow.

name: Untrusted Pointer Dereference
description: The program obtains a value from an untrusted source, converts it to a pointer, and dereferences it.

name: Use of Out-of-range Pointer Offset
description: The product performs pointer arithmetic on a valid pointer, but it used an offset that is outside the bounds of the buffer.

name: Access of Uninitialized Pointer
description: The software accesses a pointer that has not been initialized.

name: Expired Pointer Dereference
description: The software dereferences a pointer that was previously invalidated, such as after free or scope exit.

name: Improper Input Validation
description: The product does not validate or incorrectly validates input that can affect the control flow or data flow of a program.

name: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory
description: The software uses external input to construct a pathname intended to identify a file or directory below a restricted parent directory.

name: OS Command Injection
description: The software constructs all or part of an OS command using externally-influenced input, allowing command injection.

name: Cross-site Scripting (XSS)
description: The software does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes user-controllable input before it is placed in output.

name: SQL Injection
description: The software constructs all or part of an SQL command using externally-influenced input, allowing SQL injection.
code_examples: cursor.execute('SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ' + user_id)
query = f'SELECT * FROM table WHERE name = {name}'

name: LDAP Injection
description: The software constructs all or part of an LDAP query using externally-influenced input, allowing LDAP injection.

name: XML Injection
description: The software does not properly neutralize special elements that are used in XML, allowing XML injection.

name: Improper Control of Generation of Code
description: The software constructs all or part of a code segment using externally-influenced input.

name: Improper Neutralization of Directives in Dynamically Evaluated Code
description: The software receives input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements that could be interpreted as directives.

name: Improper Neutralization of Directives in Statically Saved Code
description: The software receives input but does not properly neutralize special elements that could modify the code.

name: Improper Neutralization of CRLF Sequences in HTTP Headers
description: The software receives data from an upstream component, but does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes CR and LF characters.

name: Improper Encoding or Escaping of Output
description: The software prepares a structured message for communication with another component, but does not properly encode or escape the data.

name: Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor
description: The product exposes sensitive information to an actor that is not explicitly authorized to have access.

name: Generation of Error Message Containing Sensitive Information
description: The software generates an error message that includes sensitive information.

name: Use of Hard-coded Password
description: The software contains a hard-coded password, which it uses for its own inbound authentication or outbound communication.

name: Improper Authentication
description: When an actor claims to have a given identity, the software does not prove or insufficiently proves that the claim is correct.

name: Missing Authentication for Critical Function
description: The product does not perform any authentication for functionality that requires a proven user identity.

name: Missing Encryption of Sensitive Data
description: The software does not encrypt sensitive or critical information before storage or transmission.

name: Cleartext Storage of Sensitive Information
description: The software stores sensitive information in cleartext.

name: Cleartext Transmission of Sensitive Information
description: The software transmits sensitive or security-critical data in cleartext.

name: Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm
description: The use of a broken or risky cryptographic algorithm is an unnecessary risk.

name: Use of Weak Hash
description: The product uses an algorithm that produces a digest that does not meet the expected computational effort.

name: Use of Insufficiently Random Values
description: The software uses insufficiently random numbers or values in a security context.

name: Deserialization of Untrusted Data
description: The application deserializes untrusted data without sufficiently verifying the resulting data.

name: Weak Password Requirements
description: The product does not require that users should have strong passwords, which makes it easier for attackers to compromise user accounts.

name: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference
description: The software processes an XML document that can contain XML entities with URIs that resolve to documents outside of the intended sphere of control.

name: Improper Restriction of Recursive Entity References in DTDs
description: The software uses XML documents and allows their use of DTDs, but it does not properly control the number of recursive definitions of entities.

name: Use of Hard-coded Credentials
description: The software contains hard-coded credentials, such as a password or cryptographic key.

name: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)
description: The web server receives a URL or similar request from an upstream component and retrieves the contents of this URL, but it does not sufficiently ensure that the request is being sent to the expected destination.

name: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements in Data Query Logic
description: The software generates a query intended to access or manipulate data in a data store, but it does not neutralize special elements.
